opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: This is one of the July 2025 sources where the geopolitical forecast and the metaphysical machinery fuse. The first move is concrete: NATO arms shipments resume, Trump gives Putin an ultimatum, and Israeli strikes on Iran are expected to restart. The second move is structural: America's Russia policy keeps strengthening the enemy it is supposed to weaken. The third move is the leap that gives the episode its heat: maybe Putin is not merely lucky or clever. Maybe he is being read, by himself or by the forces around him, as the world-historical figure who can control history.

beats.summary: Ukraine strategy, sanctions, and Nord Stream become three examples of American policy making Putin stronger.

paragraphs.text: The first self-defeat is Ukraine's strategy. In the counterfactual, America does not intervene, Russia overruns territory, overextends, fails at logistics, loses morale, and becomes politically vulnerable. In the actual path, Ukraine fights for every inch and thereby plays to Russia's strength: defensive artillery warfare. The harder Ukraine fights this way, the more the Russian military is forced to adapt.

paragraphs.text: The final minutes matter because they keep the episode from becoming mere apocalyptic certainty. Jiang says the next video will connect the pieces and forecast the next five to ten years. Then he names the public method: build theories and hypotheses, attach predictions, and revise the analytical model when predictions fail.
